records.ety: [OE. guise, gise, way, manner, F. guise, fr. OHG. wīsa, G. weise. See Wise, n.]

defs: Customary way of speaking or acting; custom; fashion; manner; behavior; mien; mode; practice; -- often used formerly in such phrases as: at his own guise; that is, in his own fashion, to suit himself.
quotes: The swain replied, “It never was our guise To slight the poor, or aught humane despise.”

defs: External appearance in manner or dress; appropriate indication or expression; garb; shape.
quotes: As then the guise was for each gentle swain.
A . . . specter, in a far more terrific guise than any which ever yet have overpowered the imagination.

defs: Cover; cloak; as, under the guise of patriotism.
